title: Phase 13 — Multi-agent + intervention substrate (13 sub-units)
items: 13.A Recover + index multi-agent gap-analysis doc (DONE 2026-04-30): copied /tmp/recovered-multi-agent-gap-analysis.md (extracted from session export of agent/stupefied-curie-2498c2) to archive/docs/SESSION-multi-agent-gap-analysis.md; appended index entry (id: multi-agent-gap-analysis) to archive/docs/design-archive-index.jsonl.

13.B Message<T> envelope + standard topic constants (2026-05-01): src/patterns/messaging/message.ts. interface Message<T> { id; schema?; expiresAt?; correlationId?; payload }. Standard topic constants: PROMPTS_TOPIC / RESPONSES_TOPIC / INJECTIONS_TOPIC / DEFERRED_TOPIC / SPAWNS_TOPIC. STANDARD_TOPICS tuple + StandardTopic union. JsonSchema = minimal local type (DS-13.B; zero-dep posture). 4 regression tests in src/__tests__/patterns/messaging.test.ts.

13.C selector + materialize composers (2026-05-01): src/extra/composition/materialize.ts (re-exported from composition/index.ts and top-level extra/index.ts). selector<TIn, TKey>(input, fn, opts?) — projects upstream values to routing key, deduped on projected key (custom equals; defaults to Object.is); emits ONLY when key changes. materialize<TKey, TGraph>(key, factories, parent, opts?) — reactive key + reactive factories Map mounts the matching factory's Graph under parent.mount(slotName, ...) (default 'materialized'). Re-mounts on key change. Factories mutation with same key preserves active slot per G10 deferral. Cleanup unmounts on terminate / teardown. SENTINEL when no factory matches. GraphFactory<TGraph> = () => TGraph thunk (each invocation MUST return fresh, never-mounted Graph). 11 regression tests.

13.D Recipe docs (no code) — COMPOSITION-GUIDE-PATTERNS.md (2026-05-01): three new recipes (cross-repo edit). §41 Criteria-grid verifier recipe — humanInput<{axes}> (or structured-output promptNode) → derived(.every) → approvalGate. §42 Cost-bubble recipe — parent derived over per-agent bundle.cost (preserves full TokenUsage); USD conversion via downstream derived + pricing.ts. §43 boundaryDrain recipe — locked Phase 13.J: buffer(source, notifier) covers bufferWhen semantics; alias documented; promotion criterion noted.

13.E valve + abort wiring (2026-05-01): Path (i) per DS-13.E. valve(source, control, opts?) keeps positional shape; opts widens via ValveOpts = ExtraOpts & { abortInFlight?: AbortController }. When abortInFlight supplied AND control flips truthy → falsy, valve auto-calls controller.abort(). JSDoc-locked edge cases: activation-with-falsy-control does NOT abort; already-aborted controller is no-op-safe; second close after re-open is no-op. 5 regression tests.

13.F humanInput<T> + tracker sibling presets (2026-05-01): src/patterns/orchestration/. humanInput<T>(opts: { hub, prompt, schema?, idGenerator? }) — LLM↔human runtime Q&A channel. Each prompt DATA mints fresh correlationId, publishes Message<HumanPromptPayload> to PROMPTS_TOPIC, watches RESPONSES_TOPIC for matching correlationId, emits T payload. Switchmap semantics: new prompt mid-flight abandons prior watcher. Sibling to approvalGate (design-time veto vs runtime Q&A). tracker<T>(opts: { hub, topicName?, name?, from? }) — Park-as-deferred queue consumer over DEFERRED_TOPIC (configurable). Returns { topic, subscription, pending, cursor, total, add, ack, pullAndAck }. Multi-tracker on same hub topic gets independent cursors. Total counter is keepAlive: true. Naming locked: tracker over parkedQueue / deferredTracker. 13 regression tests.

13.G AgentBundle<TIn, TOut> interface + class AgentGraph extends Graph (2026-05-01): src/patterns/ai/agents/agent.ts. AgentStatus = 'idle' | 'running' | 'verifying' | 'done' | 'error'. CostState = { usage: TokenUsage; turns: number }. addUsage(a, b) accumulator sums all token classes. AgentSpec<TIn, TOut> required name + adapter; optional systemPrompt, tools (static OR Node<readonly ToolDefinition[]>), memory, inMapper / outMapper (default identity for TIn=string / TOut=LLMResponse), maxIterations, meta. Verifier slot intentionally NOT in v1 (G7 reframe). AgentBundle: in (writable, SENTINEL until first emit, equals: () => false), out (SENTINEL until first response, equals: () => false), status, cost, graph. AgentGraph mounts inner agentLoop subgraph at loop/ + (optional) memory/. §32 mirror translates loop.status (thinking/acting) → running. Cost rollup effect accumulates via addUsage on each loop.lastResponse emit. in subscriber is F2-safe — kicks via chat.append + status.emit('thinking') rather than agentLoop.run()'s Promise bridge.

13.H agent(spec) preset + presetRegistry sugar (2026-05-01): src/patterns/ai/agents/presets.ts. agent<TIn = string, TOut = LLMResponse>(parent, spec) — mints AgentGraph, mounts under parent.mount(spec.name, ...), returns AgentBundle. presetRegistry<TPreset>(initial?) — thin sugar over reactiveMap<string, TPreset>; returns { registry, put, remove }. Generic over TPreset (decoupled from AgentSpec) so same primitive powers harnessLoop strategy registries / pipelineGraph stage catalogs / agent preset catalogs. Composes with 13.C materialize. No agent.run() per cross-cut #1 lock — caller-side runtime is bundle.in.emit(input) + awaitSettled(bundle.out, { skipCurrent: true }). 17 regression tests.

13.I spawnable() harness preset + strategy-key axis extension (2026-05-01): src/patterns/harness/presets/spawnable.ts. spawnable<TIn, TOut>(opts) mints SpawnableGraph mounted under opts.hub at opts.name (default 'spawnable'). Returns { spawnTopic, activeSlot, rejected, graph }. spawnTopic = hub.topic<Message<SpawnPayload<TIn>>>(SPAWNS_TOPIC). activeSlot: Node<ReadonlyMap<string, AgentBundle<TIn, TOut>>> — currently-mounted bundles keyed by request id. rejected: TopicGraph<SpawnRejection<TIn>> — out-of-policy requests. Per-request handler: validate (custom predicate + minimal structural check), depth-cap, expiresAt (ISO 8601), preset-registry lookup, mint via agent(this, { ...spec, name: spawn-${req.id} }), watch bundle.status → on done/error unmount + remove. Idempotent terminal-handler guard. Strategy-key axis extension (DS-13.I): StrategyKey = `${PresetId}|${RootCause}→${Intervention}` (was `${RootCause}→${Intervention}`). New PresetId = string + DEFAULT_PRESET_ID = 'default'. strategyKey(presetId, rootCause, intervention) — 3-arg signature; pre-1.0 breaking change. StrategyEntry adds required presetId field. Migrated all callsites. 11 regression tests.

13.J boundaryDrain (recipe vs factory) — locked as recipe (2026-05-01): buffer(source, notifier) already covers bufferWhen semantics. Documented as §43 boundaryDrain recipe in COMPOSITION-GUIDE-PATTERNS.md as part of 13.D landing. Promotion criterion: a second consumer with non-trivial wiring around the buffer (max-buffer-size cap, fallback emission on timeout, per-boundary TTL).

13.K G6 cross-graph explain() validation (2026-05-01) — partial confirmation, 1 gap filed: regression test src/__tests__/graph/explain-cross-mount.test.ts (5 tests). Case 1 ✅ parent → child (parent.derived ← child::node walks cleanly). Case 2 ✅ childA → parent → childB (multi-mount path through parent-level shared node by Node-ref deps). Case 3 ❌ topicBridge end-to-end: src::events → bridge::output walks; src::events → dst::events does NOT (bridge.output → target.events goes through _attachArrayToLog imperative subscribe, not a declared dep). Test pins failure mode (expect(endToEnd.found).toBe(false)); flip the assertion when the gap is fixed. Gap filed in optimizations.md 'Phase 13.K — topicBridge end-to-end explain() walk gap' with three resolution options. Defer until a real consumer hits it OR Phase 14's changesets-deltas surfaces a more general 'imperative attach' representation.

13.L G9 convergence operator landed as settle (2026-05-01): settle<T>(source: Node<T>, opts: SettleOpts<T>): Node<T> in src/extra/operators/control.ts. Forwards every upstream DATA unchanged; tracks 'quiet waves' (consecutive operator-fn invocations where upstream batch had no DATA, or DATA equal-to-prior under optional equals comparator); emits COMPLETE after quietWaves consecutive quiet waves, or after maxWaves total waves. Construction-time validation rejects non-positive-integer quietWaves / maxWaves. 8 regression tests. Boundary recap (DS-13.L): awaitSettled is one-shot Promise; settle is the reactive operator form.

13.M Worked multi-agent example test (2026-05-01): src/__tests__/patterns/ai/agents/multi-agent-example.test.ts — handoff between two agent() instances using topicBridge. Hand-rolled first using shipped primitives (messagingHub, topic, agentLoop, valve, Graph.derived/effect/state, parent.describe({ explain })) BEFORE 13.B–13.I implementation lands; serves as design lock-test. Six tests passing (handoff via topic, cost-bubble, depth-valve, cross-graph explain, SENTINEL guard, SpawnRequest envelope). Friction items F1–F9 filed in optimizations.md 'Phase 13 design-session inputs from §13.M lock-test' — motivated 13.B (Message<T>), 13.G (AgentBundle.in / .out / .cost), 13.G re-entrancy contract for agent.run under nested drains, and small keepAlive opt on GraphEffectOptions.
Verification: 2734 tests passing (+88 over Phase 12 baseline); pnpm run build green; pnpm run lint clean.
Outstanding: 13.K topicBridge end-to-end explain() walk gap (filed in optimizations.md, deferred per user direction).

title: Phase 13 — /qa pass on multi-agent batch
items: ~45 raw findings deduplicated to 13 auto-applicable patches + 1 needs-decision (N1: agent.in re-entrancy).

A1 — spawnable.validatorSchema removed (it never actually validated against the schema, only did envelope-shape checks). Kept validate?: (req) => boolean predicate as the only validation hook; Message.schema continues to flow through envelope as wire convention for caller-supplied validators (ajv / zod / valibot).

A2 — humanInput correlationId match fixed to skip retained-log replay hazard. Subscribes to responsesTopic.events and snapshots events.cache.length BEFORE publishing prompt envelope; only considers envelopes at index ≥ that cursor. Eliminates stale-envelope-with-matching-correlationId crash mode (e.g. id-generator collision across humanInput instances on a long-lived hub).

A3 — Central-clock rule: humanInput's default idGenerator and spawnable's expiresAt check route through wallClockNs() (core/clock.ts) instead of Date.now() / Date.parse() / performance.now(). Test suites that monkey-patch the central clock can pin both deterministically.
A4 — settle operator: added completeWhenDepsComplete: false so framework auto-COMPLETE on upstream COMPLETE doesn't double-fire after settle's own convergence-COMPLETE.

A5 — spawnable._processRequest: added _disposed flag (mirroring SubscriptionGraph._disposed); checked at request-handler entry AND inside per-request loop body so destroying spawnable mid-iteration doesn't try to mount agents on a torn-down graph.

A6 — StrategyEntry.presetId field: harness-loop's two strategy.record(...) calls now pass presetId: DEFAULT_PRESET_ID in extra decoration object so persisted snapshots have field populated. partial-extra hazard documented at audited-success-tracker JSDoc.
A7 — humanInput output Node: equals: () => false so structurally-equal repeat responses propagate (mirrors agent.out's precedent).

A8 — spawnable subscription: default from: 'now' so pre-existing retained spawn requests are NOT replayed at construction. Exposed as from?: 'now' | 'retained' | number opt for callers who want the replay.

A9 — spawnable per-spawn statusUnsub: now released inside onTerminal (when agent reaches done/error), instead of accumulating on SpawnableGraph's disposer list. Defensive disposer still fires on graph destroy if spawn is in-flight.
A10 — spawnable.expiresAt: invalid (non-parseable) ISO 8601 strings now reject with reason: 'invalid expiresAt' instead of silently passing through.
A11 — materialize mount/unmount: wrapped in batch() so external observers see one coherent wave for slot transition.
A12 — materialize returned Node: added describeKind: 'derived' so inspection tooling categorizes it correctly alongside selector.
A13 — humanInput envelope: schema is now carried at envelope-level only (Phase 13.B Message<T> contract), not duplicated into payload.

N1(b) — agent input queue via internal hub topic+subscription. bundle.in Node remains a writable surface, but kicks are now queued through internal topic<TIn> + cursor subscription (mounted under AgentGraph at input-topic / input-sub). Drain effect depends on [inputSub.available, loop.status] — when status is idle/done/error AND avail > 0, pullAndAck(1) and runs reset+kick batch. Mid-run inputs queue automatically; processed sequentially when loop returns to ready. Eliminates prior raw-subscribe → kick re-entrancy hazard. Two new regression tests in agent.test.ts pin behavior: 3-rapid-emit sequential ordering + per-input cost scoping under the queue.

Verification: 2734 tests pass (+4 from /qa: 2 spawnable from-cursor + invalid-expiresAt regressions, 2 agent mid-run-queue regressions; −2 from removed validatorSchema test). Build green (assertBrowserSafeBundles honored). Biome lint clean.

Deferred (not raised against this batch — minor / consumer-driven): Blind #14 rejection reason wording when custom validate fails ('validation failed' vs prior 'schema validation failed'; doc-only nit); Blind #17 spawnable depth-cap race in same drain wave (.emit updates .cache synchronously per protocol, so for-loop reads latest size; theoretical only); Blind #24 addUsage triple-evaluation perf (minor; defer until profiler shows cost rollup as hot path); Edge #4 polish (mount/unmount inside subscribe handler — sanctioned form per spec §5.9; A11 batch() wrapped); Edge #5 G10 hot-swap deferred (already documented); Edge #6 selector custom equals throw (defer; trust caller); Edge #12 closureReads separator (minor; not load-bearing for inspection today).
